myMatches.title: My matches
myMatches.subtitle: Friendly matches outside tournaments. Both players confirm the score before Elo moves.
myMatches.whatsapp_prefill: Hi {name}! Quick check on our last match — score still right?

help.why: Universal Elo only feels fair when every result is verified. This page is the verification step for matches you and a friend played outside a tournament.

what.1: Hit “Register played match” to log a match you've already finished. Pick the opponent, enter the score, you're done.
what.2: Your opponent then sees the match here under “Awaiting your confirmation” and either confirms (Elo updates instantly) or disputes it.
what.3: Matches accepted via Find a Player but not yet played show up under “Scheduled” — enter the score there once you've played.
what.4: Disputes simply clear the score so the other side can re-enter it. Cancelled matches don't change Elo.

result.1: Friendly matches feed into your universal Elo with the friendly multiplier (default 0.5×).
result.2: Both players have a paper trail — no score is ever applied without two confirmations.

empty.awaiting_my_body: When an opponent reports a score for a match with you, it appears here.
empty.awaiting_them_body: Scores you reported and that the opponent still needs to confirm appear here.
empty.scheduled_body: Accept a proposal in Find a Player or register a played match to populate this list.
empty.everything_body: Once you and a friend play and log a match, it shows up here.

card.awaiting_my_confirmation_hint: The opponent reported this score. Confirm to apply Elo, or dispute to ask them to re-enter it.
card.awaiting_their_confirmation_hint: Waiting for the opponent to confirm the score you submitted.
card.two_party_warning_inline: Your opponent will be asked to confirm before Elo changes.
card.confirm_dispute: Clear the reported score and ask the other side to enter it again?
card.confirm_cancel: Cancel this match? It won't affect Elo.
card.error_empty_sets: Enter at least one set with games played.

quick.title: Register played match
quick.subtitle: Log a friendly match you've already finished. Both players will need to confirm before Elo moves.
quick.opponent_none: No matching players visible to you.
quick.notes_placeholder: Anything worth remembering about this match
quick.two_party_warning: Heads up: your opponent must confirm this score before it counts toward Elo.

adminNav.forbidden_body: You do not have permission to view the admin area.

adminDb.title: Database
adminDb.subtitle: Direct CRUD access to every core table. Use with care — there is no soft delete.

help.why: Sometimes you need to fix a row, backfill a value or clean up test data without writing SQL. This screen exposes every main table with the same controls: filter, sort, edit, delete.

what.1: Pick a table to open its list view with search, sort and per-column filters.
what.2: Click a row to edit any non-system field; click + to insert a new row.
what.3: Use the dedicated editors (Quiz, Rating engine) for guided flows — this screen is the raw fallback.

result.1: You can recover from any data hiccup without bothering an engineer.
result.2: All changes are subject to the same RLS as the rest of the app, so referential integrity stays intact.

adminDb.new_row_subtitle: Required fields are marked with an asterisk. Empty fields become NULL.
adminDb.edit_row_subtitle: Modify any editable field and save.
adminDb.confirm_delete: Permanently delete this {table} row? This cannot be undone.
adminDb.empty_rows: No rows match the current filters.

delete_warnings.profiles_delete: This will also delete the auth.users record. The user will lose all sign-in access. All bookings, matches, ratings and reviews tied to this user will cascade-delete.

errors.cannot_delete_self: You cannot delete your own profile while signed in.
errors.insert_disabled: Inserting rows into this table from the editor is disabled.

table_help.why: Raw editor for the “{table}” table. Anything you change here is committed immediately to Postgres.

what.1: Use the search box for partial matches and the per-column filters for exact matches.
what.2: Click any column header to sort. Click again to flip direction.
what.3: The pencil opens an edit form; the trash deletes the row after confirmation.

result.1: Players, coaches and other consumers of {table} will see the change on their next page load.
result.2: Foreign-key relations are still enforced — deletes that would break references will be rejected.

adminOverview.title: Admin
adminOverview.subtitle: Configure the platform and watch its pulse.
adminOverview.section_quiz_body: Edit questions, weights and the algorithm that turns answers into a starting Elo.
adminOverview.section_rating_body: Tune K-factors, multipliers and seasonal scoring. Try changes in the simulator before activating.

help.why: Everything that drives ratings, brackets and onboarding lives in version-controlled config. You can iterate safely on a draft and switch traffic with one click.

what.1: Counters reflect the live database — including coaches, players and tournaments.
what.2: The badge on each card shows which version of the relevant config is currently in production.
what.3: Open a section to edit a draft, simulate the impact, and activate it when you are happy.

result.1: Predictable, auditable changes to the player experience.
result.2: A clear way to roll forward (or back) by version.

adminQuiz.title: Onboarding quiz
adminQuiz.subtitle: Versions, questions and answer weights for the starting-Elo questionnaire.
adminQuiz.list_subtitle: Active version drives the live onboarding flow. Drafts are isolated until activated.

help.why: The starting Elo is the very first number a new player sees. A bad guess scares people away. By tuning quiz questions and weights here, you make sure the platform meets every newcomer with a fair, friendly estimate.

what.1: Each row is a version of the questionnaire. Only one is active and shown in the public onboarding.
what.2: Click “New draft” to clone the current version (recommended) or start from scratch. Drafts can be edited freely.
what.3: Open a draft to add or remove questions, change wording in three languages and set option weights.
what.4: When the draft looks right, hit “Activate” — the next signup will use it immediately. Old answers stay attached to the version that produced them.

result.1: Newcomers get a starting Elo that matches their real level, so they enjoy their first matches instead of being smashed.
result.2: You can iterate without code: ship a question on Monday, tweak weights on Friday, all from this screen.

adminQuiz.confirm_delete: Delete this draft? This cannot be undone.
adminQuiz.empty_body: Create your first draft to start collecting starting-Elo answers.
adminQuiz.dialog_create_body: Drafts are private until activated. Cloning copies all questions; you can then tweak them.
adminQuiz.clone_explainer: All questions and weights from the chosen version will be copied.
adminQuiz.notes_placeholder: Why are you making this version?
adminQuiz.locked_active: This is the active version. Clone it to create a new draft for editing.

errors.active_version_locked: Cannot edit an active version. Clone it into a new draft.
errors.version_has_no_questions: Add at least one question before activating.
errors.version_has_answers: This version already has player answers and cannot be deleted.

help.why: Every question contributes a weight to the player's starting Elo. Tweak text, options and weights without touching code.

what.1: Reorder questions with the arrow buttons — order is what the player sees.
what.2: Single/multi-choice options award the listed weight when selected.
what.3: Scale uses (answer − center) × coef; numeric uses linear or step-based formula.
what.4: Activate a version to point /onboarding/quiz at it.

result.1: A localized, weighted questionnaire that produces a fair starting Elo.
result.2: Safe iteration: previous versions stay in the database with their own answers.

question.list_count: {n, plural, =0 {No questions yet} =1 {1 question} other {# questions}}
question.empty_body: Add the first question to start shaping the starting Elo.
question.number_hint: Use "coef" for a literal value, or "coef from config" to pull a number out of the active rating config (e.g. start_elo.experience_per_year).

adminRating.title: Rating engine
adminRating.subtitle: Tune how Elo is calculated for matches and onboarding.
adminRating.list_subtitle: Each version is an immutable snapshot. Activate one at a time.
adminRating.empty_body: Create the first draft to define K-factors, multipliers and seasonal scoring.
adminRating.dialog_create_body: Cloning starts from an existing version's values; otherwise sane defaults are used.
adminRating.clone_explainer: All values from the chosen version will be copied as a starting point.

help.why: Universal Elo decides who plays whom in a fair match, who lands in the seasonal race, and how big a win feels. This screen is your safe place to evolve those rules — nothing is editable in code.

what.1: Each row is one snapshot of the rating engine. Only one is active and feeds the live system.
what.2: Click “New draft” to clone the active version. Open the draft to change K-factors, multipliers, starting Elo, and seasonal scoring.
what.3: Use the built-in simulator on the editor page to type in two players + a result and see exactly what Elo deltas this draft would produce — no need to play a real match.
what.4: When you are happy, press “Activate”. From that moment on, every new match score and every onboarding quiz uses the new values; the old version stays in history.

result.1: You can fine-tune rating dynamics gradually — e.g. make tournament finals weigh more without breaking friendly matches.
result.2: If a change feels wrong, just clone the previous version, activate it, and you are back to the old behaviour in one click.

editor.section_start_elo_hint: Used by the onboarding quiz to pick a fair initial Elo.
editor.section_k_factors_hint: Higher K = faster Elo change. Lower K = more stable.
editor.section_multipliers_hint: Scales every Elo delta by the kind of match.
editor.section_season_hint: Defines length and scoring for the seasonal race.
editor.season_scoring_hint: Map of event code → points awarded for the seasonal race.
editor.confirm_activate: Activate this version? It will replace the current production rating engine.
editor.locked_active: This is the active version. Clone it to edit.

simulator.title: Match simulator
simulator.hint: Try the configuration against a hypothetical match before activating.

coachLeaderboard.title: Player leaderboard
coachLeaderboard.subtitle: Universal Elo rating across all rated matches.
coachLeaderboard.note_recent_30d: Δ columns aggregate rated-match changes over 7 / 30 days.
coachLeaderboard.empty_mine_title: No players linked to you yet
coachLeaderboard.empty_mine_description: Players appear here once they book one of your slots or join one of your tournaments. Switch to All players to see the wider directory.
coachLeaderboard.empty_all_description: When players register and opt in to the leaderboard, they will appear here ranked by Elo.

help.why: Track how your players progress and spot upcoming talent in your district.

what.1: My players — anyone who has booked a slot with you or played in your tournament.
what.2: All players — every player who is visible in the leaderboard, ordered by Elo.
what.3: Δ7d / Δ30d — net Elo change from rated matches in the last 7 / 30 days.

result.1: A clear picture of who is improving fastest in your circle.
result.2: A way to identify players ready for tougher tournaments or coaching plans.

hero.eyebrow: A tennis platform from a pro
hero.title: Your tennis ranking. No compromises. With a touch of Federer's style.
hero.subtitle: One Elo rating that follows you everywhere. Find an opponent, play a tournament, check coaches — in one place.

rating.title: Universal ranking
rating.body: One Elo for all your matches — friendlies, tournaments, leagues.

find.title: Find an opponent
find.body: Filter by district, level and your free time. A match in two clicks.

coaches.title: Coaches with reviews
coaches.body: Real ratings from players who actually trained with them.

tournaments.title: Tournaments your way
tournaments.body: Best-of-3, super-tiebreak, timed match, first-to-X-games. You choose how to play.

quote.text: “Federer's backhand isn't just technique — it's a philosophy. Let's build a ranking we'd be proud to show.”
quote.author: — inspired by the one-handed backhand (and a 6'8" / 203 cm reach).

hero.tagline: One rating. Every match. Zero compromises.

pillars.title: Three pillars. One platform.
pillars.subtitle: Everything you need to take your tennis seriously — in one place.

rating.name: Book a coaching session
rating.body: Pick a coach matched to your level and goals, and book a court in two clicks. The right mentor turns weeks of guesswork into measurable progress — and bad habits gone for good.

find.name: Find a player
find.body: Filter by district, level and your free time. A match in two clicks, contact on WhatsApp.

tournaments.name: Tournaments for every taste
tournaments.body: Best-of-3, super-tiebreak, timed match, first-to-X-games — pick the format, we'll run the bracket. Tournaments in every format: weekend events, evening series or a one-day grind.

helpDemo.title: Help components in action
helpDemo.subtitle: Every admin page must use these. This is where you sanity-check how they look.

panel.why: This is a demo page. It shows how HelpPanel introduces the coach to the meaning of each view.

what.1: See how collapsing works (state stored in localStorage).
what.2: Inspect the three-block layout: Why / What / Result.
what.3: Copy the pattern into your admin page.

result.1: The coach never has to guess what a button does.
result.2: Less support, more trust.

tooltip.intro: Hover or tap the “?” icon next to a term to see a short explanation. Examples:

empty.title: Silence on court
empty.description: Nothing here yet. Every empty state in the app must have purpose and a CTA — like this one.

login.title: Welcome on court
login.subtitle: Sign in with a password or an email magic link.
login.help_magic: Click the link in the email to sign in. It might land in spam — peek there too.
login.help_signup_confirm: We sent a confirmation link to your email. Open it to activate the account.
login.forgot_sent_body: We sent a password reset link. Click it to set a new password.

updatePassword.title: Set a new password
updatePassword.subtitle: Pick a strong one — at least 8 characters.
updatePassword.done_body: You can now use your new password. Redirecting you…
updatePassword.no_session: The link expired or is invalid. Please request a new reset link from the sign-in page.

coachDashboard.title: Coach dashboard
coachDashboard.subtitle: Your command center. Everything happening in your club, in one place.

help.why: See what's happening in your club right now: upcoming sessions, fresh bookings, active tournaments, average review.

what.1: Jump into the next training session in one click.
what.2: Accept new bookings (if manual confirmation is enabled).
what.3: See how many people signed in for the first time today.

result.1: “Confirm booking” will email + Telegram-ping the player.
result.2: Creating a new tournament opens the 5-step wizard.

today.title: Today's sessions
today.empty: No sessions today. Time for a coffee ☕

pending.title: Pending match confirmations
pending.empty: Nothing pending. All matches are confirmed.

coachDashboard.forbidden: You don't have access to the coach dashboard.

s1.title: Welcome aboard
s1.body: This is your dashboard. Start with the obvious: invite your first players. Without players, the rest is just a fancy spreadsheet.

s2.title: Open up your court
s2.body: Create venues, courts, and recurring slots. Players will book themselves — you just review the calendar each morning.

s3.title: Run a tournament in 5 minutes
s3.body: Pick a format (single-elim or round-robin to start), set match rules, add participants — and the bracket draws itself.

s4.title: Polish your public profile
s4.body: Add a bio, hourly rate, and pin your location on the map. Players reading reviews will find you faster.

s5.title: Stuck? There's a glossary
s5.body: Elo, no-ad, swiss, K-factor — every term has an in-app definition and FAQ entry.

empty.title: Start by inviting a player
empty.description: A club without players is like a racket without strings. Invite your first five — it takes 30 seconds.

quiz.title: Your starting rating
quiz.subtitle: A quick quiz (2 minutes) to estimate your level. Your first 10 matches will fine-tune it with extra weight.

help.why: Every player needs a starting point. Estimating from answers beats starting from zero.

what.1: Answer honestly — the system adapts anyway.
what.2: You can go back and change answers before submitting.
what.3: After submit, you'll see your rating in the top-right corner.

result.1: We'll set your initial Elo (range 800–2200).
result.2: We'll enable a “provisional” period — first 10 matches count more.

done.title: Your start
done.body: Your first 10 matches count with extra weight — the system will quickly correct your true level.

coachPlayers.title: My players
coachPlayers.subtitle: Manage your club: invite, import, track invitation status.

help.why: Your players list. Invite new ones and manage the club from here.

what.1: Send an email invitation (one player at a time).
what.2: Import a list from CSV (coming soon).
what.3: Check who already accepted and who hasn't.

result.1: An invitation creates a one-time link valid for 14 days and sends an email.
result.2: On accept, the player goes through the quiz and joins your club.

invite.title: Invite a player
invite.subtitle: Type the email. First/last name optional but help with identification.

recent.title: Recent invitations
recent.empty_description: Your first invitations will show up here. Average accept time is 2 days.

invite.title: {coach} invites you
invite.body: After signing in you'll go through a 2-minute quiz to set your starting Elo, then to your player dashboard.

help.why: This page accepts a coach invitation. After sign-in your account will be linked to their club.

what.1: Sign in via magic link (or Google).
what.2: Take the starting quiz.
what.3: Start playing, booking courts and finding partners.

result.1: The coach will see you on their players list.
result.2: The invitation will be marked accepted (one-time).

invalid.title: Invalid invitation
invalid.body: The link is invalid or has already been used.

expired.title: Invitation expired
expired.body: Ask your coach to send a new one.

already.title: Invitation already accepted
already.body: Sign in normally from the login page.

rating.title: My rating
rating.subtitle: Your Elo, recent match history and the seasonal race for prizes.

help.why: This is where you see how your true level changes with every match. One rating — all matches.

what.1: Check current Elo and the 30-day delta.
what.2: See how Elo grew / dropped on the chart (last 20 matches).
what.3: Track the seasonal race and the top coaches leaderboard.

result.1: After each confirmed match Elo updates automatically.
result.2: First 10 matches count more — the system quickly finds your true level.

history.title: Elo history
history.empty: No matches yet. The chart will appear after your first game.

coaches.title: Top coaches
coaches.subtitle: by player ratings
coaches.empty_description: The top-20 coaches will show up here once players start leaving reviews.

quiz_cta.title: Take the quiz to set your starting rating
quiz_cta.body: Until you answer a few quick questions about your experience, your Elo is the default (1000). The quiz takes ~2 minutes and gives you your true starting point.
quiz_cta.bullet2: You get a personalised starting Elo (~800–2200).
quiz_cta.bullet3: Matches will still fine-tune your rating — the quiz just gets you to your level faster.

find.title: Find a player
find.subtitle: Sparring partners in your district, at your level, in your free time slots.

help.why: In Poland the hardest part is finding a partner of similar level who is free at the same time. We do that for you.

what.1: Filter by district, Elo range, dominant hand, and your preferred time slots.
what.2: Results are ranked by match score (Elo + overlapping slots + same district + recent activity).
what.3: You can either jump straight to WhatsApp or send a formal in-app match proposal.

result.1: Sent proposals reach the other player by email; they can accept or decline.
result.2: Once accepted, the match shows up in “My proposals” — coordinate on WhatsApp and enter the score.

filters.title: Filters
filters.elo_radius_hint: We'll show players within ±{n} Elo of yours.
filters.availability_hint: Tap windows when you can play. Slots must overlap with the partner's.

results.empty_description: Try widening the Elo range, adding more districts, or removing preferred slots.

card.overlap_none: No overlapping slots — coordinate on WhatsApp instead.
card.duplicate: You already have an active proposal with this player.
card.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

find.whatsapp_prefill: Hi {name}! From Bury Tennis — got time for a match?

my_availability.title: My schedule
my_availability.hint: Your standing “when I'm generally free to play” slots. Saved on your profile and used to surface you to others.
my_availability.empty_hint: Nothing set yet. Mark the windows you're usually free — without this almost no one will find you.

proposals.title: My proposals
proposals.subtitle: Incoming, sent, and history.
proposals.empty_incoming_description: When someone proposes a match to you, it'll show up here.
proposals.empty_sent_description: Open “Find a player” and send your first proposal.

help.why: All your friendly-match proposals in one place — so nothing slips through.

what.1: Accept or decline incoming proposals in one click.
what.2: Cancel sent ones if your plans change.
what.3: Message your partner directly on WhatsApp at any time.

result.1: Once accepted, the match becomes “scheduled” and is waiting for the score.
result.2: After the score is entered, your Elo updates automatically.

card.whatsapp_prefill: Hi {name}! About the Bury Tennis match…

help.why: Your profile. Everything the system knows and shows other players — you can change it here.

what.1: Edit personal info and contacts.
what.2: Set sport preferences and availability (needed for “Find a Player”).
what.3: Manage privacy and notifications.

result.1: Changes are visible immediately.
result.2: Some fields (e.g. health) are visible only to a coach with whom you have an active booking.

hints.date_of_birth: Used only for age statistics in rankings & tournaments.
hints.motto: Short motto shown next to your name in “Find a Player”.
hints.phone: Backup number (call / SMS). Visible to a coach you have an active booking with.
hints.whatsapp: The de-facto messenger in Poland. Clicking “Message” opens a WhatsApp chat with a partner or coach.
hints.telegram_username: Optional. Without @. Only if you prefer Telegram over WhatsApp.
hints.district: District is key for finding opponents near you.
hints.backhand_style: One-handed vs two-handed — helps opponents match style.
hints.availability: Mark when you usually play. “Find a Player” will match others in the same slot.
hints.visible_in_find_player: Turn off if you don't want match proposals.
hints.visible_in_leaderboard: Turn off to hide from the public leaderboard.
hints.notification_email: Primary channel for automated notifications (invitations, booking confirmations, tournament results).
hints.notification_whatsapp: Coming soon: automated notifications via WhatsApp Business. For now WhatsApp is used for direct contact.
hints.notification_telegram: Optional. Requires linking your account with the Telegram bot.
hints.health_notes: Injuries, allergies — visible only to your current coach.

avatar.bad_mime: Unsupported format. Use JPG, PNG, WebP or GIF.
avatar.requirements: JPG / PNG / WebP / GIF, up to 2 MB.

venues.title: Venues & courts
venues.subtitle: Manage the locations and courts where you teach. They are reused later when you publish training slots and schedule tournaments.

help.why: A venue is a club or facility where you teach. Each venue has one or more courts. You'll attach training slots and tournament matches to specific courts, so this is the foundation of your schedule.

what.1: Create a venue: enter its name, city/district and amenities (lights, indoor, parking, etc.).
what.2: Open the venue and add its courts: number, surface and current status (active or under maintenance).
what.3: If a court is being repaired, set it to 'Maintenance' — it will be hidden from new bookings automatically.

result.1: Players will see a clear address with amenities when booking.
result.2: When you create a slot or tournament match, you only need to pick a court — date and place are pre-filled.

list.empty_description: Add your first location — it'll only take a minute, and unlocks slot publishing and tournaments.
list.delete_confirm: Delete this venue and all its courts? This cannot be undone.
list.courts: {n, plural, =0 {no courts yet} one {# court} other {# courts}}

hints.lat_lng: Optional. Used for the venue map (Phase 2). You can copy coordinates from Google Maps.
hints.address: Visible to players. Helps with navigation.
hints.amenities: Tap to toggle. The list is shown to players when they book.

help.why: Each court is the smallest schedulable unit on the platform. Slots, training sessions and tournament matches are all attached to a specific court.

what.1: Add the courts physically present at this venue: number them the way they are signposted on site.
what.2: Pick the surface — players filter venues by surface type.
what.3: Use 'Maintenance' for courts under repair. They stay in the system but won't appear in new bookings.

result.1: When a slot conflict happens, the system will warn you with the exact court number.
result.2: Tournament draw can spread matches across multiple courts to save time.

courts.title: Courts at this venue
courts.intro: Add every court at this venue. The 'Number' is what you'll use during a tournament to direct players (e.g. 'Match 12 → Court 3').
courts.empty: No courts here yet — add the first one below.
courts.name: Name (optional)
courts.delete_confirm: Delete this court? Existing slots will be removed too.
courts.duplicate: A court with this number already exists at this venue.

slotsCoach.title: Training slots
slotsCoach.subtitle: Publish open slots and let players book them. Use weekly recurrence to plan a whole month in seconds.

help.why: Slots are the bookable units of your schedule. Each slot lives on a specific court and can be individual, paired (1+1) or a group lesson.

what.1: Click 'Add slots' and choose either a one-off date or a weekly recurrence (pick weekdays + how many weeks ahead).
what.2: Players see open slots in 'Bookings' and book them. You'll see participants here.
what.3: Cancelling a slot also cancels all bookings on it — a notification will be sent to players (Phase 2).

result.1: Your week fills up with bookings without any back-and-forth messaging.
result.2: Conflicting times are blocked automatically by the database — you can't double-book a court.

slotsCoach.empty_description: Pick another week or publish a few slots — players are waiting.
slotsCoach.cancel_confirm: Cancel this slot? All confirmed bookings will be cancelled too.
slotsCoach.no_courts_description: You need at least one venue with a court before you can publish slots.

dialog.title: Add slots
dialog.intro: Pick a court, choose one-off or weekly recurrence, and we'll create everything in one go.

hints.weekly: We'll create a slot for each picked weekday across the chosen weeks.
hints.price: Optional. Leave empty for free / per-club pricing.
hints.max_participants: 1 for individual, 2 for pair, more for group.

dialog.result_created: {n, plural, =0 {Nothing created} =1 {1 slot created} other {# slots created}}.
dialog.result_conflicts: {n, plural, =1 {1 conflict skipped} other {# conflicts skipped}} — open to see details
dialog.no_courts: Add a court to your venues first.

bookings.title: Bookings
bookings.subtitle: Find an open slot with your coach (or a new one) and book it in two clicks.

help.why: Bookings replace messaging your coach to ask 'are you free Wednesday at 6?'. The slot you see here is guaranteed to be available right now.

what.1: Pick a date range and (optionally) district. Click Search.
what.2: Tap Book on the slot you want. Your coach is notified by email; you can also message them on WhatsApp from your booking card.
what.3: Need to cancel? Hit Cancel on your booking — earlier the better, your coach can free the slot for someone else.

result.1: You see all your upcoming sessions in one place.
result.2: Coach knows in advance how many people are coming.

search.title: Find a slot
search.intro: Search across all coaches in the platform.
search.empty_description: Tap Search to load open slots.
search.no_results: No open slots in this window. Try a wider range or a different district.

bookings.no_upcoming: No upcoming bookings yet — find a slot below.

card.notes_placeholder: Anything I should know? (injury, late arrival, …)
card.whatsapp_prefill: Hi {name}! It's about my Bury Tennis booking — quick question:

tournamentsCoach.title: Tournaments
tournamentsCoach.subtitle: Run your club tournaments end-to-end: create, register players, draw, score.

help.why: A clean tournament page replaces messy chat threads, paper brackets and lost scores. Every player sees the same single source of truth.

what.1: Create a tournament — pick a format (single elimination is supported now; the rest are coming) and match rules (best-of-3, super-tiebreak, timed match, etc.).
what.2: Add participants from your club; you can also let players self-register if you set the tournament to public.
what.3: Generate the bracket. We handle byes, seeding (by Elo / random / manual) and walkovers automatically.
what.4: Enter the score after each match. The winner advances, the bracket updates, and the tournament finishes itself when the final is decided.

result.1: Players know exactly when, where and against whom they play.
result.2: You spend the day coaching, not chasing scores in WhatsApp.

list.empty_description: Create your first tournament — best-of-3, single elimination is a great start.
list.delete_confirm: Delete this tournament? Participants and matches will be removed.

hints.format: Single elimination is supported in MVP; other formats are coming soon.
hints.privacy: Public tournaments appear in /me/tournaments → Open registrations for any player.
hints.draw_method: How to assign positions in the bracket. You can override per draw.
hints.match_rules: Defines how a single match is played. Used as a default; you can override per match later.

participants.title: Participants
participants.empty: No participants yet — add players above.
participants.no_options: No matching players in the directory.

bracket.title: Bracket
bracket.regenerate_warning: A bracket already exists. Generating again will erase all current matches. Continue?
bracket.no_matches: No bracket yet. Add at least 2 participants and click Generate.
bracket.not_supported: This format will be available in a future iteration. For now, use single elimination.
bracket.insufficient_players: Need at least 2 participants to draw a bracket.

help.why: This page is your control room: participants on the left, bracket (or schedule for round-robin) on the right. Update both, players see changes instantly.

what.1: Add or remove participants until you're happy with the field, then click Generate bracket.
what.2: After each match, click Score on the match card and enter set-by-set results.
what.3: Single-elim auto-advances winners; round-robin auto-finishes when every match has a result and standings update live.

result.1: A finished bracket — or final standings table — is a single, shareable view of how the tournament played out.
result.2: Every result feeds the universal Elo with the correct multiplier.

standings.title: Standings
standings.empty: Standings will appear once matches are scored.

tournamentsPlayer.title: Tournaments
tournamentsPlayer.subtitle: Find an open tournament, register, and track your bracket.

help.why: All your tournaments — past, present and open for registration — live here.

what.1: Open registrations: tap Register on any open tournament. The coach gets your name automatically.
what.2: My tournaments: see the next match you have to play and your opponent.
what.3: Need to drop out? Use Withdraw — earlier is better, so the bracket can be redrawn.

result.1: You never miss a tournament you wanted to play.
result.2: You always know who you play next.

tournamentsPlayer.open_empty_description: Coaches haven't published any public tournaments. Check back soon!
tournamentsPlayer.mine_empty_title: You're not in any tournament yet
tournamentsPlayer.mine_empty_description: Browse Open registrations to find one to join.
tournamentsPlayer.withdraw_confirm: Withdraw from this tournament? Your spot will be released.
tournamentsPlayer.no_next_match: Nothing scheduled yet — bracket may still be in the works.

coachesPublic.title: Coaches
coachesPublic.subtitle: Browse coaches in the network — book lessons, read reviews, find your match.

help.why: Choosing the right coach is half the result. Real reviews from real students cut through the marketing noise.

what.1: Default sort uses a Bayesian-weighted average — five reviews at 5★ won't outrank fifty at 4.85★.
what.2: Switch to “Raw stars” or “Most reviewed” to slice the list differently.
what.3: Open a coach to read reviews, see hourly rate and write your own after a real lesson.

result.1: Trustworthy podium of top coaches each week.
result.2: Great coaches climb fast; weak ones get the feedback they need to grow.

coachesPublic.reviews_count: {count, plural, =0 {no reviews} one {# review} other {# reviews}}
coachesPublic.empty_body: When coaches join the platform they will appear here.

detail.self_view_hint: This is your own coach profile. Reviews are written by your students.
detail.empty_reviews_body: Be the first to share your experience after a lesson or tournament.

help.why: Reviews must be honest. Players can post one review per real interaction (lesson or tournament).

what.1: See aggregate stars and recent text reviews.
what.2: If you've worked with this coach, the review form will appear below.
what.3: Coaches can reply publicly to keep the conversation healthy.

result.1: A trustworthy reputation system instead of empty marketing.
result.2: Constructive feedback that helps coaches grow.

source_hint.booking: Eligible because of your confirmed booking.
source_hint.tournament: Eligible because of your tournament participation.

review_form.text_placeholder: Share what worked, what could be better — keep it constructive.
review_form.success: Review saved. Thanks for the feedback!

errors.missing_stars: Please pick a star rating first.
errors.not_authenticated: Please sign in to leave a review.
errors.invalid_payload: Some fields look off — please check and retry.
errors.not_eligible: You can review a coach only after a confirmed lesson or tournament with them.
errors.db_error: Something went wrong on our side. Please retry.

myCoaches.title: My coaches
myCoaches.subtitle: All coaches you've worked with — share your honest feedback.

help.why: Honest reviews help other players pick the right coach and help great coaches stand out.

what.1: Each card shows a coach you had a confirmed lesson or tournament with.
what.2: Tap the green button to write or update your review.
what.3: Reviews can be edited any time; status reflects moderation state.

result.1: Coaches receive direct, source-of-truth feedback.
result.2: The community gets a more trustworthy coach leaderboard.

myCoaches.empty_body: After your first confirmed lesson or tournament, the coach will show up here.

adminReviews.title: Coach reviews moderation
adminReviews.subtitle: Keep the reputation system honest. Hide spam, flag disputes, remove abuse.

help.why: Reviews carry a coach's reputation — moderation prevents abuse from both sides.

what.1: Use the filter to focus on flagged or hidden items.
what.2: Publish to make a review visible; hide to take it down without deleting; remove for clear violations.
what.3: Aggregate stars and counts on profiles are recomputed automatically.

result.1: A healthier coach marketplace players can trust.
result.2: Clear audit trail for every moderation action.

adminReviews.empty_body: When new reviews come in, they will appear here.
adminReviews.confirm_remove: Remove this review for good? This cannot be undone.

coachesMap.title: Coach map
coachesMap.subtitle: {count, plural, =0 {No coaches on the map yet} one {# coach pinned} other {# coaches pinned}} — click a marker to see details.
coachesMap.empty_body: Coaches who add their location to their profile will appear here.

help.why: A map answers the most basic player question: who's actually nearby?

what.1: Markers show coaches who opted into the public map and set coordinates.
what.2: Marker badge shows current star rating; click to open the full profile.
what.3: If you're a coach, set your pin in Coach panel → Public profile.

result.1: Players quickly find coaches in their district.
result.2: Coaches with a pin get more visibility — geography matters in tennis.

coachProfile.title: Public coach profile
coachProfile.subtitle: Tune what players see when they open your profile or the map.

field.show_on_map: Show me on the public coach map

placeholder.bio: Tell players who you are: experience, training style, age groups…

hint.rate: Optional. Players see this on your card; leave empty if it varies.
hint.map: Search a place or click the map to drop a pin. You can drag the pin to fine-tune.
hint.show_on_map: Turn off if you'd rather not appear on the public map (your profile remains accessible).

map.hint: Click the map or drag the pin to set your location.
map.none: Place not found — try a different query.

errors.invalid_payload: Some fields look off — please check and retry.
errors.rate_out_of_range: Hourly rate must be between 0 and 10000.
errors.latlng_must_be_paired: Set both latitude and longitude (or clear the pin).

helpPage.title: Help & glossary
helpPage.subtitle: Everything you need to know about ratings, formats and how the platform works.

help.why: Newcomers to club tennis often hit the same dozen questions. This page lives to answer them once.

what.1: Glossary of all the in-app jargon — Elo, K-factor, Swiss, no-ad…
what.2: FAQ for the most common everyday actions.
what.3: Direct contact channel if anything's still unclear.

result.1: Less back-and-forth with the coach about how things work.

elo.def: A number that estimates your level. Goes up when you beat better players, down when you lose to worse ones.

k_factor.def: How much your Elo can move per match. Higher for new players (more uncertainty), lower for veterans.

provisional.def: Your first 10 matches. K is double, so the system zeroes-in on your real level fast.

starting_elo.def: Your initial rating, computed from a 60-second quiz when you sign up.

race.def: A leaderboard counted within a season window. Top N players win prizes; details on the Rating tab.

tournament_seed.def: Your slot in the bracket based on rating. Higher seeds avoid each other in early rounds.

bye.def: An automatic free pass to the next round when the bracket size doesn't divide evenly.

no_ad.def: Deuce point becomes sudden-death: receiver chooses side, winner takes the game. Saves time.

super_tiebreak.def: First to 10, win by 2 — instead of a full third set.

wo.def: Win awarded when an opponent doesn't show up. Counts for advancement, not for Elo.

match_proposal.def: A friendly-match request between two players. Both sides confirm before Elo updates.

verified_coach.def: Coach with at least 5 published reviews — earns a green Verified badge.

trust_weighted.def: Coach ranking that shrinks high averages with few reviews back toward the global mean.

outbox.def: Internal queue of all emails. The cron worker drains it every few minutes.

q_register.a: Either accept your coach's invitation email, or sign up directly with magic-link / Google.

q_no_invite.q: I don't have an invitation — can I still join?
q_no_invite.a: Yes. Sign up directly, take the starting quiz, and contact a coach via WhatsApp from their public profile.

q_dispute.q: We disagree on the score — what now?
q_dispute.a: Either side can mark the score as disputed. The coach reviews and decides; Elo only updates after that.

q_payment.a: MVP: manual tracking. The coach marks bookings as paid / unpaid / comped. Online payments are coming in Phase 2.

q_cancel.q: How do I cancel a booking?
q_cancel.a: Open My Bookings → Upcoming → Cancel. You can cancel up to 12 h before the session by default.

q_telegram.a: Poland skews heavily toward WhatsApp. Telegram is a secondary channel — toggle it on in your profile.

q_data.a: Stored on Supabase EU servers. Export & delete on request. We never sell data.

helpPage.contact_body: Email hello@bury.tennis or WhatsApp the coach directly from their profile.

tournamentsPublic.title: Public tournaments
tournamentsPublic.subtitle: Browse open tournaments across the network. Sign up if you like the format.

help.why: Lets anyone — guests included — see what's happening across the platform: who plays where, when, and which formats are popular.

what.1: Filter by status: upcoming, in progress, finished.
what.2: Click a tournament to see the bracket and participants.
what.3: Sign in to register, set your starting Elo via the quiz, and join.

result.1: Builds organic trust: even a guest can verify a coach's track record.
result.2: Players see the calendar at a glance and plan their season.

empty.title: No tournaments here yet
empty.description: Try a different tab — or, if you're a coach, create one in your dashboard.

help.why: A read-only public view of one tournament — players, bracket, scores, results.

what.1: See who's seeded where and follow the draw round-by-round.
what.2: Check live scores once matches kick off.
what.3: Share the link — no login required to view.

result.1: Pros and parents alike can follow along without an account.

footer.tagline: Universal Elo, find a player nearby, run tournaments. Tennis in your pocket.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: Questions, feedback or a bug? Drop us a line — we reply within 24 h.

install.title: Install the app on your phone
install.body: Open it like a native app — no App Store or Google Play. Works offline after the first visit.
install.android_step_2: Tap the menu (⋮) in the top-right corner.
install.android_step_3: Choose "Install app" or "Add to Home screen".
install.android_native_hint: Your browser supports inline install — no extra steps required.
install.ios_step_1: Open this page in Safari (not Chrome or an in-app browser).
install.ios_step_2: Tap the Share icon at the bottom of the screen.
install.ios_step_3: Choose "Add to Home Screen" and confirm.

accountSecurity.title: Password & security
accountSecurity.subtitle: Change your password directly, or email yourself a reset link.
accountSecurity.link_mode_hint: We will send a one-time link to your inbox. Open it, pick a new password, and you're done.
accountSecurity.toggle_to_link: Forgot your password? Send me a reset link
accountSecurity.toggle_to_direct: I know my current password — change it here
accountSecurity.success_changed: Password updated. Use the new one next time you sign in.
accountSecurity.success_link_sent: Reset link sent. Check your inbox (and spam folder).
accountSecurity.min_hint: Minimum 8 characters. Use a unique combination.
